page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the DSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable to support external storage attachments.

Benchmark results position the D48s_v5 as a solid performer across multiple workloads. It demonstrates strong capabilities in memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 2503.54 GB/sec. CPU performance is robust, highlighted by a strong rating in PassMark CPU encryption tests and image processing. Multi-core performance is supported by hyper-threading, yielding a Geekbench multi-core score of 15438.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 46633.34. For specialized tasks, the instance achieves a strong text generation speed of 285.41 tokens per second using a small 135M parameter LLM.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48s_v5 offers a balanced resource density for enterprise workloads that require dedicated compute resources without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The lack of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring users to provision external Premium Storage, which allows for tailored storage configurations. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory operations, CPU encryption, and image processing, alongside solid multi-core processing. The architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. The D48s_v5 provides a cost-effective balance of compute and memory resources for enterprise applications. It is highly suitable for workloads such as web serving, database operations, software compilation, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability, delivering strong memory and encryption performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong memory and encryption benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering dedicated Intel Xeon compute power and strong memory performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Strong benchmark performance in memory operations and CPU encryption
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Optimized for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
